data.title: ASML, other semi equipment makers, dip after MATCH Act reaches US Congress
data.description:
ASML (ASML), along with other semiconductor equipment manufacturers, showed slight declines during early Tuesday trading after a draft version of the bipartisan MATCH Act reached the U.S. House of Representatives.

ASML was down 1.5%, while Lam Research (LRCX), Applied Materials (AMAT), and KLA (KLAC) had all inched down less than 1%.

This follows the introduction of the Multilateral Alignment of Technology Controls on Hardware last week, which intends to strengthen national security by closing gaps in export controls on semiconductor manufacturing equipment by U.S. allies such as the Netherlands-based ASML.

"China has made it abundantly clear that it intends to dominate the technologies that underpin both our economy and our national defense," said U.S. Congressman Michael Baumgartner (R-WA). "The United States cannot afford to leave open back doors that allow the Chinese Communist Party to acquire the tools it needs to leap ahead in semiconductor manufacturing. I introduced the MATCH Act to ensure that America and our allies move in lockstep to close these gaps, defend our technological edge, and safeguard the supply chains that power everything from our weapons systems to our critical infrastructure. This is about protecting American workers, American innovation, and American security for the long haul."

The analysts at Citi Research said this development is a negative for ASML and could lead to "significant unintended consequences."

"We understand the desire of the US and many other Western nations of limiting China's access to semiconductor technology, particularly as it relates to artificial intelligence," said Citi analysts Andrew Gardiner and Daniel Schafei in a Tuesday investor note. "The existing restrictions, both on equipment (no EUV tool shipments to China) and AI chips, have significantly limited Chinese companies' ability to develop their own AI chips."

"If ASML was restricted from selling DUV immersion but not DUV dry tools into China, that would limit the ability of Chinese chipmakers to build out new production lines in their fabs," they added. "China does not today have the capability of producing its own lithography tools, so without ASML immersion tools, China's most advanced foundries and memory chip makers would be limited in their ability to expand production. We would expect an impact not only to ASML in such an outcome, but all chip equipment makers who sell into China, including the major US makers."

ASML generated 29% of its revenue from China in 2025, Citi noted. The US semiconductor equipment manufacturers also generated 30% to 40% of revenue from China last year. If the MATCH Act passes, Citi expects China would likely retaliate, leveraging its key position in the mining and refinement of rare earth minerals.

The MATCH Act specifically targets China's largest fabs, such as those run by ChangXin Memory Technologies, Hua Hong, Huawei, Semiconductor Manufacturing International, and Yangtze Memory Technologies, including all subsidiaries and affiliates. 

ASML is slated to report its first quarter 2026 financial results on April 15. A consensus estimate expects the company to report earnings per share of $7.67 on revenue of $9.99B. During the same quarter last year, ASML reported EPS of $6.83 and revenue of $8.82B.

data.title: AC Immune rises after amending licensing deal with Lilly
data.description:
AC Immune (ACIU) traded higher on Tuesday after the Swiss biotech announced an amendment to a license and collaboration agreement signed with Eli Lilly (LLY) in 2018 to develop a new class of neurology therapies known as Morphomer Tau.

Under the original agreement signed in December that year, the Indiana-based pharma giant agreed to develop Morphomer Tau aggregation inhibitors in partnership with AC Immune (ACIU) for neurodegenerative diseases, including Alzheimer’s.

The amendment continues to cover R&D work related to new lead Tau Morphomer candidates and potential backup compounds, AC Immune (ACIU) said, adding that the company will begin investigational new drug ((IND))-enabling studies as part of the deal in H1 2026.

In addition to the payments it is already eligible for under the existing agreement, the amendment allows the company to receive a CHF 10M payment upfront and a milestone payment when Phase 1 dosing begins.

data.title: Retail options flows turn defensive as put demand surges
data.description:
Retail investors are growing more cautious in the options market, even as overall activity remains elevated, according to new flows data from Citadel Securities.

Positioning has shifted decisively toward defense over the past week, with flows skewing 4% toward selling based on Citadel's Call/Put Direction Ratio — marking the first bearish tilt since late November, according to Scott Rubner, head of equity and equity derivatives strategy.

Demand for downside protection has accelerated sharply. Put buying has reached extreme levels, with 5-day rolling purchases hitting record highs. Notably, this surge in hedging has occurred during periods of market strength. Between March 27 and April 2, when the S&P 500 (SPY) (IVV) (VOO) rose 1.6%, retail investors spent more than $275M on net put premium — the largest 5-day total in nearly a year.

 

 

The data suggest a shift in mindset rather than participation, with retail maintaining high activity levels while increasingly positioning for potential downside risks.

Flows have also rotated away from single-stock options toward broader market hedges. Retail investors are now trading more than 1.5 index and ETF options for every single-stock contract, a marked departure from the long-run average closer to parity.

Rubner said the shift toward index hedging reflects growing concern about macro risks, as investors look to protect portfolios against broader market moves rather than express conviction in individual names.

data.title: Food phenomenon: GLP-1 usage is forecast to hit 15% of the U.S. population
data.description:
The latest report from Morgan Stanley on GLP-1 adoption in the U.S. indicates that usage is accelerating, with oral formulations and expanded access expected to unlock a broader user base over the next several years. The firm now forecasts that 19M patients in 2026 and 55M in 2035 (15% of the population) will consistently be on an anti-obesity medication. Notably, the forecast is a significant increase from prior estimates.

Morgan Stanley's refreshed U.S. calorie model points to a -1.6% reduction in total calorie consumption by 2035, largely unchanged despite higher penetration forecasts. "While we expect the headwind to food demand to continue to build over the next several years as adoption accelerates (~40e bps impact through 2025 with another ~100–150e bps over time), the long-term impact remains broadly unchanged vs. our prior forecasts as higher adoption is offset by real-world usage patterns, including lower persistence, weight regain, and more moderate behavior among incremental users," update the firm.

Of interest, Morgan Stanley's 4th AlphaWise survey of GLP-1 users in the U.S. continues to confirm meaningful changes in consumption behavior, with ~50% of users reducing calorie intake by more than 20% via fewer meals/snacks or smaller portions. As the GLP-1 user base expands toward lower-body mass index cohorts, behavior increasingly skews toward more moderate and selective changes. However, that is still a significant headwind for many food and beverage companies. 

Morgan Stanley analyst Megan Clapp and her team continue to view GLP-1 adoption as part of the broader health and wellness shift, representing an ongoing pressure point for food companies. In the beverage sector, analysts Dara Mohsenian and Eric Serotta think the key for beverage companies will be innovating to zero-calorie products, which is already being seen with non-alcoholic beverages and zero-sugar energy drinks. For the restaurant sector, GLP-1 usage is seen as a headwind for fast food and a tailwind for full-service and healthier fast-casual chains. For the grocery sector, analyst Simeon Gutman and his team project the impact of GLP-1 adoption as a modest headwind to grocery volume, although pharmacy growth provides a partial offset.

Some of the stocks seen by Morgan Stanley as being most at risk are Dollar General (DG), Dollar Tree (DLTR), and BJ's Wholesale (BJ), while the least at-risk chains are expected to be Costco (COST), Albertsons Companies (ACI), and Kroger (KR). Moderate risk is seen for Target (TGT), Walmart (WMT), and Grocery Outlet (GO).

It is already well documented that General Mills (GIS), Mondelez International (MDLZ), PepsiCo (PEP), Conagra Brands (CAG), Kraft Heinz (KHC), Kellanova (K), Hershey (HSY), Coca-Cola (KO), McDonald’s (MCD), and Starbucks (SBUX) face incremental sales pressure from increased GLP-1 usage.

data.title: Centerspace raised to Buy-equivalent at Piper Sandler
data.description:
Centerspace (CSR) stock was raised to Overweight from Neutral at Piper Sandler on the back of its recent underperformance.

"Despite the Iran War and spike in oil prices, we don't see themes changing much for REITs heading into 1Q26 earnings, with our expectation for retail and industrial to lead, apartments to lag, and NYC office to show unappreciated strength," said analyst Alexander Goldfarb.

"REITs outperformed the broader market in 1Q26, with the group +3.8% YTD versus S&P 500 -4.6%. Jobs remain a focus, with March's +178K standing in contrast to February's -133K (revised lower from initial -92K), and YTD monthly average of 68K versus 2025's ~15K monthly pace," said Goldfarb.

"The 10-year stands at 4.3%, which is essentially where it's been since 3Q23. While the economic and political debate of a prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz rages, our bigger concern is a potential metastasization of private credit, given an infection of the financing market is not good for CRE," said the analyst.

The investment bank attributed the upgrade on CSR to underperformance versus apartment peers YTD (-10% vs. -6.5%).

"Its Upper Midwest portfolio offers investors differentiated exposure than the Coasts or Sunbelt of peers. While Denver (20% of NOI) contends with oversupply, CSR's smaller markets remain strong," said the research note.

data.title: Apple dips on report of production delays for first foldable iPhone
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) is seeing setbacks in the engineering test phase of the foldable iPhone and could push back its production and shipment schedule, Nikkei Asia reported. Shares in the Cupertino, California-based company fell 4.3% in morning trade. 

Issues surrounding the engineering development of foldable iPhones are more complex and are taking more time to resolve than Apple had expected, and in the worst case scenario could delay the first shipment by months, according to multiple sources briefed on the matter.

A few component suppliers, though not all, have been notified that there is a possibility the component production schedule for the foldable iPhone will be pushed back, the report said.

"It's true that more issues than expected have emerged during the early test production phase, and additional time will be needed to resolve them and make necessary adjustments. ... The current situation could put the mass production timeline at risk," one of the people familiar with the matter said.

"April will mark a crucial stage of the engineering verification test, and this month till early May is extremely critical," the person added.

Apple (AAPL) has adjusted its iPhone launch strategy for 2026, pushing back production of the standard model to early 2027 in order to prioritize premium models, including foldable iPhones, and to better allocate constrained supplies of memory chips and other key resources, Nikkei Asia reported earlier.

However, another person with knowledge of the situation said the potential schedule delay has nothing to do with the components and materials crunch but is more about the engineering challenges of the American tech giant's first foldable iPhone.

"Apple and the supply chain are working under a pressured timeline, and the current solutions are not enough to completely solve the engineering challenges. ... More time is needed," the person added.

Apple (AAPL) has planned to initially produce about 7 million to 8 million foldable iPhones, and the foldable iPhone accounts for less than 10% of the company's initial planned production volume for this year's new lineup, the report added.

Samsung Electronics (SSNLF) and Huawei, Apple's two major rivals, have launched generations of foldable smartphones since 2019.

Updates to add stock price at 10:25 am.

data.title: Eni makes 2 Tcf gas discovery offshore Egypt
data.description:
Eni (E) said Tuesday it made a significant gas and condensate discovery offshore Egypt after drilling the ​Denise W‑1 exploration well in the Temsah ‌concession in the Eastern Mediterranean, which will help the company support Egypt's ​goal of boosting gas reserves and increasing production.

Preliminary estimates indicate ~2 Tcf of gas initially in place and 130 barrels of associated condensates.

The Denise W discovery lies 70 km offshore in 95 m of water depth and less than 10 km from existing infrastructure, enabling substantial synergies for a fast-track development, the company said.

Eni (E) operates the Denise development lease with a 50% working ⁠interest ​alongside BP (BP), with operations conducted ​through Petrobel, Eni's joint venture with Egypt's state-owned oil company ​EGPC.

data.title: Uber leans on AWS chips to speed ride and delivery matches, train AI
data.description:
To speed up rider and delivery matching and enhance its infrastructure and artificial intelligence capabilities, Uber Technologies (UBER) is expanding its use of Amazon Web Services’ (AMZN) Gravitron4 and Trainium3 processors.

"Uber operates at a scale where milliseconds matter," said Kamran Zargahi, vice president of engineering at Uber. "Moving more Trip Serving workloads to AWS gives us the flexibility to match riders and drivers faster and handle delivery demand spikes without disruption.”

By moving more of these applications to Gravitron4, Uber (UBER) can reduce energy costs while scaling rapidly during peak demand. 

In addition to increased reliance on AWS’ Gravitron4, Uber (UBER) has also begun experimenting with AWS Trainium3 chips to train some of the AI models that help power its apps. These models analyze data from billions of rides and deliveries to determine which driver or courier to send, calculate arrival times, and recommend the best delivery options to the customer in a more cost-effective manner.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Tuesday: HUM, UNH, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged lower on Tuesday as geopolitical tensions intensified following reports of U.S. military strikes on Iran's Kharg Island. The targeted operation at the strategic oil hub comes as President Donald Trump warned of "imminent destruction" for the nation if it refuses to agree to a ceasefire.

Here are some of Tuesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Humana (HUM) +4% – Medicare insurers rallied after the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services said it will raise average Medicare Advantage payments by 2.48% for 2027, amounting to more than $13B in additional funding. The move lifted peers, including Elevance Health (ELV) +3%, UnitedHealth Group (UNH) +8%, CVS Health (CVS) +3%, and Centene Corporation (CNC) +1%, as investors priced in improved reimbursement outlooks.
  • Broadcom (AVGO) +3% – Shares gained after announcing a long-term deal with Alphabet (GOOG/GOOGL) to develop future generations of tensor processing units (TPUs) through 2031, alongside a supply agreement for networking and AI rack components. The companies also partnered with Anthropic to deliver multi-gigawatt TPU capacity starting in 2027. Citi analyst Atif Malik flagged potential upside to Broadcom’s $100B–$130B AI revenue target while reiterating a Buy rating and $475 price target.

Biggest stock losers

  • Mach Natural Resources LP (MNR) -10% – Shares declined after the company announced a secondary offering of ~9M common units by existing unitholders, including VEPU, Simlog, and Sabinal Energy Operating. The company will not issue new units or receive any proceeds from the sale. The offering includes a 30-day option for underwriters to purchase up to an additional 1.35M units, though completion of the transaction is not guaranteed.
  • Tractor Supply (TSCO) -3% – Shares fell after BofA reinstated coverage with a Neutral rating and a street-low $447 price target, with analyst Christopher Nardone citing a more uncertain demand backdrop following Q4 results amid geopolitical tensions and rising fuel costs. The firm also flagged persistent headwinds, including soft pet spending, weak big-ticket demand, and sluggish discretionary trends, which are expected to limit upside to earnings.

data.title: Perrigo gains amid takeover speculation
data.description:
• Perrigo Co. (PRGO) rose as much as 7.5% amid speculation that the generics drug maker may be a takeover target. 
  • Germany-based Stada Arzneimittel AG may be interested in Perrigo (PRGO), according to traders, who cited a Betaville "uncooked" alert that was circulating on Monday. 
  • Perrigo (PRGO) has short interest of 11.5%  and a market cap of $1.46 billion.
